label: plugin/config conflict
evidenceLines: [Server thread/ERROR]: Failed to load plugin BadJar v1.0
java.util.zip.ZipException: invalid CEN header
safeFirstStep: Stop accepting players, snapshot `plugins/BadJar`, and replace the corrupted jar before restarting.
likelyCause: Corrupted plugin jar file triggered by a bad upload.
customerMessage: BadJar couldn't load because its jar is corrupted; I'm replacing it with a known-good build before restarting.
resolutionNotes: Swapped in a fresh jar; the plugin loads cleanly afterward.

label: plugin/config conflict
evidenceLines: Loading plugin LegacyNetBridge v2.3.1
java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: io.netty.channel.ChannelFuture
safeFirstStep: Stop the server, snapshot `plugins/LegacyNetBridge` and bundled libs, and deploy a build targeting the current Paper API.
likelyCause: Plugin bundles Netty 4.0 while Paper already removed the referenced class.
customerMessage: LegacyNetBridge references an old Netty class; I'm matching the plugin build to the running server before restarting.
resolutionNotes: Rebuilt the plugin against Paper's Netty; the NoClassDefFoundError disappears.

label: plugin/config conflict
evidenceLines: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Expected Boolean
Loading plugin ChunkGuard v3.4
safeFirstStep: Stop the server, back up `plugins/ChunkGuard/config.yml`, and revert the invalid setting or let the plugin regenerate defaults.
likelyCause: Malformed YAML in ChunkGuard's config prevented it from parsing new fields.
customerMessage: ChunkGuard's config contains an invalid Boolean; I'm restoring the previous config before starting.
resolutionNotes: Corrected the config syntax; ChunkGuard loads successfully afterward.

label: startup error
evidenceLines: [Server thread/ERROR]: Encountered an error during world load
java.net.BindException: Cannot assign requested address
safeFirstStep: Stop the JVM, change `server-ip` to `0.0.0.0` or blank, and restart so the container can bind.
likelyCause: `server-ip` is hard-coded to a host-local address that the container cannot reach.
customerMessage: The configured server-ip can't bind inside the container; I'm clearing it before we restart.
resolutionNotes: Reset `server-ip` to blank; server binds successfully on the bridge interface.

label: likely infrastructure issue
evidenceLines: [Server thread/WARN]: Failed to bind to port 25565
Address already in use
safeFirstStep: Drain traffic, run `ss -tulpn | grep 25565`, and identify the legacy process holding the port before restarting.
likelyCause: Another host-level service is already listening on 25565, so Paper can't bind.
customerMessage: Port 25565 is occupied by another process; I'm stopping that process before the server comes online.
evidenceToCollect: `docker compose ps` to ensure no duplicate containers
resolutionNotes: Stopped the conflicting service, confirmed the port was free, and restarted the server.

label: startup error
evidenceLines: Error: Unable to access jarfile /data/paper-server-old.jar
[init script] Starting server with NO_FILE permission
safeFirstStep: Stop the wrapper, correct the jar path (or rename the file), and restart once permissions check out.
likelyCause: Wrapper still points to an outdated `paper-server-old.jar` that no longer exists.
customerMessage: The startup script refers to a jar that isn't there; I'm updating the path before retrying.
resolutionNotes: Renamed the jar/matched the script; the server starts without issues.

label: startup error
evidenceLines: java.lang.UnsupportedClassVersionError: com/example/Main has been compiled by a more recent version of the Java Runtime (class file version 61.0)
This server requires Java 17
safeFirstStep: Stop the service, point the wrapper to Java 17, and rerun `java -version` before restarting.
likelyCause: Startup script still invokes Java 11 while Paper requires Java 17.
customerMessage: Paper needs Java 17; I'm switching the runtime before bringing it back online.
evidenceToCollect: Full `commands` line from the failed start
resolutionNotes: Repointed to Java 17; the UnsupportedClassVersionError disappears.

label: version mismatch
evidenceLines: This world was saved with Minecraft 1.20.4
This server supports up to 1.19.4
safeFirstStep: Stop the container, collect `world/level.dat` and `paper.yml` target version, and coordinate with the team owning the newer release before downgrading.
likelyCause: World files come from a newer release than the server binary supports.
customerMessage: The world uses 1.20.4 format; I'll align the server/world before restarting.
resolutionNotes: Rolled the server back to 1.20.4 or exported the world for the newer release; load succeeds once versions match.

label: plugin/config conflict
evidenceLines: [Server thread/ERROR]: Detected circular dependency between LoopHook and LoopHook
Loading plugin LoopHook v1.0
safeFirstStep: Stop the server, remove one of the circularly-dependent plugins from `plugins/`, and restart.
likelyCause: LoopHook is configured to load-after itself, creating a dependency cycle.
customerMessage: LoopHook declares a circular dependency, so I'm isolating one of the plugins before restarting.
evidenceToCollect: Stack trace around the circular dependency
resolutionNotes: Adjusted the plugin metadata; Paper loads without the loop.

label: startup error
evidenceLines: Paper encountered an error while reading paper.yml
[Server thread/INFO]: Reloading server (attempt 2/5) after fatal config error
safeFirstStep: Halt the container, restore a clean `paper.yml`, and only restart after confirming the file loads without repeated loops.
likelyCause: Corrupted `paper.yml` confuses Paper and triggers a restart loop.
customerMessage: The config file is corrupted; I'm restoring a sane copy before retrying.
resolutionNotes: Replaced `paper.yml` with the clean template; the server stops looping and starts successfully.

label: startup error
evidenceLines: Wrapper failed: required environment variable WORLD_NAME is not set
Error: Unable to find world folder
safeFirstStep: Source the wrapper's required env vars or provide them via `.env`, then rerun the wrapper.
likelyCause: Wrapper script expects `WORLD_NAME` and `JAVA_HOME`, which are missing in the env file.
customerMessage: The wrapper needs specific environment variables; I'm setting them before restarting.
resolutionNotes: Populated the required env vars; the wrapper starts the JVM normally.

label: likely infrastructure issue
evidenceLines: [Server thread/ERROR]: java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space
Container 3GB memory limit reached (Pterodactyl enforces headroom)
safeFirstStep: Stop traffic, collect `docker inspect`/`docker stats`, and either lower `MAX_MEMORY` or request more container headroom.
likelyCause: MAX_MEMORY requests 6G but the container limits heap to 3G, so the JVM OOMs.
customerMessage: The container only allows 3G but we asked for 6G; I'm adjusting the limits before restarting.
resolutionNotes: Matched MAX_MEMORY to the container quota; the JVM now starts without OOMs.

label: likely infrastructure issue
evidenceLines: Failed to bind to 0.0.0.0 because Docker assigned 10.0.0.5
Connection refused when probing 127.0.0.1
safeFirstStep: Pause the load balancer, document Docker/Pterodactyl IP allocations, and align the server's `server-ip` and port with the allocated address before restarting.
likelyCause: Server continues to bind to 127.0.0.1 while Pterodactyl allocates 10.0.0.5:25575.
customerMessage: Networking is misaligned with the assigned IP; I'm checking the allocation and updating the server binding before we re-run.
resolutionNotes: Aligned the server binding; the container now accepts traffic on 10.0.0.5:25575.

label: plugin/config conflict
evidenceLines: [Server thread/ERROR]: java.lang.NoSuchMethodError: org.bukkit.event.player.PlayerLoginEvent.getPlayer()
Loading plugin BetterJoin v1.4
safeFirstStep: Stop the server, quarantine the outdated plugin, and install a build tested against this Paper release.
likelyCause: BetterJoin was built for Paper 1.18 and calls removed APIs.
customerMessage: BetterJoin is referencing deprecated APIs; I'm swapping in the matched build before we restart.
evidenceToCollect: Plugin release notes showing compatible Paper versions
resolutionNotes: Updated to a Paper 1.20-compatible build; the NoSuchMethodError disappears.

label: needs escalation
evidenceLines: The server has stopped responding. This crash report has been saved.
Escalate to platform: insufficient evidence to determine root cause.
safeFirstStep: Stop the JVM if it keeps spinning, gather the crash report and `docker inspect`, and escalate with the collected evidence.
likelyCause: Ambiguous crash with no clear stack trace requires higher-level escalation.
customerMessage: The crash report lacks enough detail, so I'm escalating to infrastructure with the collected logs.
resolutionNotes: Escalated to platform; they confirmed the host stack and provided a fix.
